title: quercus ilex var. microphylla
intro: Quercus ilex var. microphylla, also known as the dwarf holm oak, is a small-leaved variety of the holm oak tree (Quercus ilex) native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and tannins. The health benefits of Quercus ilex var. microphylla include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall well-being. Quercus ilex var. microphylla can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine.
